1.11a: Contact point for standard documents is the Issuing Office shown on the front of the Mortgage Offer.
1.11b: Contact point for inappropriate standard documents is the Issuing Office shown on the front of the Mortgage Offer.
1.7: Lending is not permitted where the borrower and mortgagor are different entities.
1.14: A firm may act if the person handling the transaction or their immediate family is the seller, provided a different fee earner or partner of equal standing acts for the lender.
There must be no conflict of interest and Law Society guidelines must be followed.
1.15: A firm may act if the person handling the transaction or their immediate family is the borrower, provided a different fee earner or partner of equal standing acts for the lender.
There must be no conflict of interest and Law Society guidelines must be followed.
3.1.3: Notification of the seller's solicitor or licensed conveyancer's name and address is not required.
3.1.4: If the seller does not have legal representation, contact the Issuing Office.
3.1.5: Acceptable List A ID: Signed Employer's Photo ID card (if employer is known to the conveyancer) or a UK-issued debit/cheque guarantee card with an original account statement less than 3 months old.
Acceptable List B ID: Inland Revenue Notice of Tax Codes less than 12 months old or a Home Office Residence Permit for EU Nationals.
3.2.3: Notification of the seller's legal representative's details is not required.
3.2.4: Contact the Issuing Office if the seller is unrepresented.
4.5a: Contact Originations if a re-inspection of the property is required.
4.5b: The Certificate of Title must be sent to Originations.
4.1: A copy of the valuation report for the applicant is provided with the instructions.
4.3: Contact Originations if the valuer's assumptions are found to be incorrect.
5.1.1: Lending is generally prohibited for sub-sales, back-to-back transactions, or contract assignments.
Refer to the Issuing Office at least 2 weeks before exchange if the seller has owned the property for less than 6 months.
Required details for referral include: previous vendor details, purchase price paid by the current vendor, connections between parties, work carried out, and transaction dates.
5.2.1: Refer all cases where the seller is not the registered owner (outside standard exceptions) to the Issuing Office with full details.
5.4.4: Environmental, flood, or contaminated land reports do not need to be sent to the lender unless a local search identifies unremedied contamination.
Inform the lender if the property has an EPC rating of F or G.
Climate Hazard Reports: only report Medium/Moderate or High exposure risks.
Radon Gas: Area-wide reports are not required, but specific high radon levels for the property must be referred to the lender.
Mining: Reports showing a mine entry or adit within 20 metres or a history of subsidence claims must be submitted with an interpretive and buffer report.
Mundic: Properties in postcodes PL10-18, PL22, and PL23 require a Class A certificate. Class A/B requires referral; Class B or C is declined. Pre-1965 builds without a report must be referred.

Fire Risk Assessment (FRA): Required for flats. The FRA must be in-date. Outstanding works and associated costs for the leaseholder must be reported to the Issuing Office at least 2 weeks before exchange.
5.4.5: Personal searches are accepted at the conveyancer's risk and must include appropriate insurance covering both lender and borrower.
Chancel Repair: No insurance required if there is no liability at Land Registry and the property was transferred for value after 13 October 2013.
Chancel Repair: Insurance is required for first registrations or known liabilities.
5.4.6: Search insurance is not accepted for second-hand purchases; a local search is mandatory.
Search insurance is not required for remortgages.
New builds: Search insurance is accepted if the original plot search has expired.
Delayed search insurance is permitted subject to specific conditions regarding planning consents and land charges.
5.5.3a: Report building or planning consent breaches to the Issuing Office; an indemnity policy does not negate the need for a valuer's check.
Report planning conditions related to flood defences; indemnity insurance is not acceptable for unsatisfied flood conditions.
New build flats: Verify construction complies with Building (Amendment) Regulations 2018; refer if older regulations apply.
New builds require building control sign-off at or before completion.
5.5.3b: Original or copies of planning permissions should be retained by the borrower.
5.5.3c: Building regulation consents are not required by the lender.
5.5.3d: Certificates of lawful use or development are not required by the lender.
5.5.4: Restrictions affecting value or marketability should be reported to Originations.
5.7.1a: Flying freeholds are acceptable if they only affect part of the property; 100% flying freeholds are not accepted.
5.7.1b: Freehold flats are not accepted, except for coach house flats above garages or access ways.
5.7.1c: Report accepted flying freeholds or coach house flats to Originations.
5.8.1: Security comprising a building with up to four flats where the borrower occupies one and owns the freehold is acceptable.
5.8.5: Reciprocal leasehold/freehold arrangements for two flats are acceptable; both the leasehold and freehold interests must be charged.
5.10.1: Report restrictions on use to the Issuing Office.
Breaches of restrictive covenants must be reported with specific details including wording, date, owner, and potential enforcement risks.
5.13.1: Deposits sourced from crypto-assets (even if converted to fiat) must be reported to the Issuing Office.
5.14.15a: Absentee or insolvent landlords must be reported to Originations.
5.14.15b: Indemnity insurance for absent landlords is acceptable for up to 6 flats in a building; buildings with 7 or more flats are unacceptable.
Leasehold houses with absent landlords are acceptable with indemnity insurance.
Indemnity limit must be the higher of the purchase price or property value.
5.14.17a: Conveyancers must refer LPE1 Form answers (Q8.19 & 8.20) indicating building safety issues to the Issuing Office before exchange.
A1 rated EWS1 forms do not need to be reported.
The lender may provide Building Safety certificates; missing documents must be obtained from the seller, management company, or freeholder.
5.14.17b: Conveyancers must verify the accuracy of Leaseholder and Landlord Certificates against the lease agreement and available evidence.
Specific checks are required for leaseholder qualifying status as of 14 February 2022.
Details of the last sale prior to 14 February 2022 must be obtained (unless not on HMLR).
Refer to the Issuing Office if the leaseholder or landlord is determined to be non-qualifying under the Building Safety Act 2022.
5.14.1: Minimum unexpired lease term is 70 years at start and must have 30 years remaining at the end of the mortgage term.
Second-hand lease: Unacceptable if Ground Rent > 0.5% of value, doubles more frequently than every 20 years, is compounded RPI, or has a review period of 5 years or less.
Second-hand lease: Refer if Ground Rent is between 0.1% and 0.5% of value or if Estate Rent Charges exceed £500 per annum.
New build lease: Must have a minimum term of 125 years (flat) or 250 years (house).
New build lease: Ground rent must be peppercorn only; any review mechanism is unacceptable.
Lease extensions must be completed under the Leasehold Reform Housing and Urban Development Act 1993.
5.14.10: Report service charge increases exceeding £1000 per annum to the Issuing Office.
Report planned works or Section 20 notices with cost estimates and timescales.
5.14.11: Indemnity insurance for unsatisfactory lease terms must be referred to the Issuing Office.
A Deed of Variation is preferred for missing Mortgagee Protection Clauses; indemnity is a last resort requiring explanation.
5.14.12: Ground rent and service charge receipts should be retained by the borrower.
5.14.13: Evidence of notice service should be retained by the borrower.
5.15.2a: Non-regulated rent charges for estate upkeep must include a requirement for the collector to notify the lender before taking action for non-payment.

Rent charge agreements must prohibit the creation of a lease or ensure lease surrender upon payment of arrears and reasonable costs. A Deed of Variation is required if these terms are missing (indemnity is not accepted).
5.15.2b: Management company share certificates should be retained by the borrower.
5.15.2c: Signed blank stock transfer forms do not need to be sent to the lender.
5.15.2d: Management company Memorandum and Articles of Association do not need to be sent.
5.16.2: Contact Originations if unable to certify that a search entry does not relate to the property.
5.17.5a: Powers of Attorney do not need to be sent to the lender.
5.17.5b: A statutory declaration of non-revocation of power of attorney must be sent to the lender.
5.19.1: Contact Originations for requirements regarding affordable housing, shared equity, and shared ownership.
5.20.1: If solar panel lease terms do not meet UK Finance minimums, a Deed of Variation is required; otherwise, the case cannot proceed.
5.20.4: Green Deal loans with a balance over £10,000 must be reported with details of the balance, payments, works, and EPC.
6.4.4: All incentives, including guaranteed rental payments, must be reported.
6.6.1: Rent back arrangements are not accepted.
Report if the existing tenant or seller retains any interest in the property.
Selective licensing must be in place or confirmed to be granted by completion.
6.6.3: Standard ASTs up to 36 months do not require the tenancy agreement to be sent.
Non-standard tenancies (e.g., to Corporate bodies, LAs, Universities) must be submitted for review and meet specific occupancy and repossession rights criteria.
6.6.4: HMOs are only acceptable if expressly stated in the mortgage offer and require a valid licence.
6.7.1: Acceptable warranties include NHBC, LABC, Premier Guarantee, BLP, Build-zone, Checkmate (Castle 10), and others listed; CRL is accepted only under specific underwriter conditions.
6.7.4: A Professional Consultant's Certificate (PCC) is acceptable for new builds in developments of up to 10 units (or 4 units in a terrace/block) and for conversions/refurbishments under 5 years old.
6.7.6: The Professional Consultant's Certificate must be sent to the Issuing Office.
6.8.1: Unadopted roads are acceptable for new builds if adoption is planned or managed by residents.
For second-hand properties, report unadopted road details to the Issuing Office, including maintenance obligations and disputes.
Septic tanks must comply with General Binding Rules; works must be completed before mortgage completion (no retentions).
6.9.1: Indemnity insurance is not accepted if the property has no legal access.
Simultaneous formalization of access rights at completion is acceptable without referral.
6.10.2: Retentions or instalments are released to the Solicitor or Licensed Conveyancer.
6.11.1: Report redevelopment or road proposals to the Issuing Office; HS2 impact does not need reporting.
6.13.1: Outstanding improvement or repair grants must be reported to the Issuing Office with terms and amounts.
7.3: Consent to mortgage from occupants aged 17 or over is not required for Buy-to-Let mortgages.
9.1: Acceptable indemnity policies must be noted on the Certificate of Title with the insurer and policy number.
Indemnity insurance is not required for cash gifts.
9.2: The limit of indemnity must be the higher of the full purchase price or property value.
10.2b: Minimum notice for funds is 5 working days, or 10 working days if a re-inspection is required.
10.7: Return advance funds within 3 working days electronically if completion is delayed.
10.11: Interest at the contract rate is charged if the return of funds is delayed.
14.1.5: Original mortgage deeds and title documents do not need to be sent to the lender.
14.2.2: After completion, send the Official Copy of the register to the Title Registration Team in Northampton or via the specified email address.
16.3.7b: Standard attestation: 'Signed as a deed by The Mortgage Works (UK) PLC by two duly authorised officials'.
17.2.1b: Discharge is via DS1 form sent to Accounting Servicing.
